executive_summary: Our research on social engineering-driven crypto hacks ($17B lost, 65% of breaches) and institutional financial institutions now monitoring crypto more aggressively than exchanges dominated coverage, positioning us as the authoritative voice on both security threats and compliance infrastructure. Stablecoin adoption narratives (potential $1.5 quadrillion annual processing by 2035) and agentic payment mainstreaming (100M+ Base transactions) reinforced our thought leadership on payment system evolution and institutional surveillance demand.

market_landscape.title: Institutional TradFi Convergence Accelerating
market_landscape.body: Wall Street is adopting crypto playbooks (lobbying, product innovation), Binance added US stock/ETF trading, and Coinbase rolled out Indian rupee pairs. Our institutional surveillance tools are becoming table stakes for any TradFi player entering crypto; adoption is no longer optional.

market_landscape.title: Compliance Standards Rising, Gaps Persist
market_landscape.body: 47% of 2026 crypto firms now meet old top compliance standards; institutional monitoring outpaces exchange vigilance. We are the measurement authority for this trend, but the data also reveals persistent compliance asymmetry that creates ongoing demand for our products.

market_landscape.title: Bitcoin Volatility & Geopolitical Risk Premium
market_landscape.body: Bitcoin fell to 6-week lows amid Iran conflict jitters and ETF outflows ($2.8B bleeding streak); macro headwinds and geopolitical uncertainty are reasserting price discovery. This volatility increases institutional demand for risk management and transaction surveillance tools.

competitor_reads.Arkham Intelligence: Positioned as Mt. Gox blockchain tracker and real-time on-chain data provider; their coverage of legacy exchange rehabilitation signals competitive focus on historical risk narratives and on-chain transparency, not compliance infrastructure.
competitor_reads.Elliptic: Emphasizing DeFi compliance governance models and institutional partnerships (Tapbit) to scale compliance-as-infrastructure; directly competitive on institutional positioning but less visible on sanctions enforcement and threat intelligence.
competitor_reads.TRM Labs: Positioning their Solutions Architecture team around agentic AI tools (Mycroft); signaling competitive investment in automation and AI-native compliance workflows—area where we should validate our own automation roadmap maturity.
competitor_reads.Solidus Labs: Partnering with B2C2 on institutional market integrity; focused on crypto-native market surveillance and manipulation detection—complementary to our work but narrower institutional scope.
